title: Phelipanche hohenackcri
intro: Phelipanche hohenackeri, also known as Hohenacker's broomrape, is a parasitic flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Phelipanche hohenackeri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, particularly those related to the skin and respiratory system.
